1.title: Sunrise City
1.description: A bright, propulsive pop track with shimmering synths and an upbeat vocal hook that captures the feeling of a city waking up. Best for mornings, commutes, or anything that needs an optimistic boost.

2.title: Midnight Coding
2.description: Slow-tempo lofi beats layered over a warm vinyl crackle. Designed for deep focus — the repetitive rhythm fades into the background without demanding attention. A go-to for late-night work sessions.

3.title: Storm Runner
3.description: A hard-driving rock track built around distorted power chords and a punishing drum pattern. The energy peaks in the chorus with a wall-of-sound guitar lead. Fits high-intensity workouts or frustration-releasing moments.

4.title: Library Rain
4.description: Hushed lofi production with rain sounds woven into the mix. The low tempo and soft piano melody create a contemplative, slightly melancholy mood — ideal for rainy days, reading, or quiet reflection.

5.title: Gym Hero
5.description: A punchy, high-BPM pop track with driving bass and an anthemic chorus built to push through the last reps of a workout. High energy without the rave-style drops of pure EDM.

6.title: Spacewalk Thoughts
6.description: Drifting ambient textures with long reverb tails and no discernible beat. The mood is weightless and introspective — like watching stars. Works well for meditation, sleep prep, or background listening during creative work.

7.title: Coffee Shop Stories
7.description: Smooth, relaxed jazz with brushed drums and a clean acoustic guitar line. The tempo is conversational — not too sleepy, not too lively. Tailor-made for a café date, dinner background, or unwinding after work.

8.title: Night Drive Loop
8.description: A moody synthwave track with pulsing arpeggios and a nostalgic 80s production aesthetic. The pace feels like cruising through neon-lit streets at midnight. Fits late-night drives or focused creative sessions.

9.title: Focus Flow
9.description: Minimal lofi production optimized for sustained concentration. The groove is steady and unobtrusive, with subtle chord changes that keep it interesting without breaking focus. A reliable study companion.

10.title: Rooftop Lights
10.description: Indie pop with jangly guitars and an airy vocal that feels like a summer night with friends. Energetic enough to dance to, warm enough to be the soundtrack of a good memory.

11.title: Broken Highway
11.description: Melancholy country-folk with fingerpicked acoustic guitar and a worn, honest vocal. The lyrics evoke long drives through empty landscapes and the weight of things left behind.

12.title: Circuit Breaker
12.description: High-octane EDM with a relentless build and a euphoric synth drop. The kick drum hits at 140 BPM and the energy never lets up. Made for peak workout moments, pre-game hype, or festival main stages.

13.title: Velvet Soul
13.description: Smooth R&B with lush chord progressions, warm bass, and a silky vocal delivery. The tempo is relaxed but the groove has a subtle pulse that keeps it from feeling sleepy. Good for unwinding or a chill evening in.

14.title: Cathedral Echo
14.description: A sparse classical piece with long reverb that recreates the acoustic of a large, empty hall. The melody is simple and repeating — deeply peaceful and contemplative. Ideal for quiet focus or stress relief.

15.title: Street Cipher
15.description: Confident hip-hop with sharp lyricism, a heavy 808 bass, and a beat that commands attention. The mood is assertive and self-assured — fits a pre-interview pump-up, a morning commute, or anything that needs an edge.

16.title: Rust and Rain
16.description: Alternative rock with a slow-burn tension — clean guitar verses that open into heavy, distorted choruses. The mood is melancholy and introspective, sitting between sadness and resolve.

17.title: Neon Serenade
17.description: A romantic synthwave track with a sweeping lead melody and lush, layered pads. The production is modern but emotionally warm — fits a date night, a slow drive, or a scene from an 80s movie.

18.title: Dusty Porch Blues
18.description: Traditional blues with slide guitar, a shuffling rhythm, and a voice that sounds like it has lived through a few hard seasons. Slow, honest, and emotionally resonant — best for reflective solitary listening.

19.title: Mountain High
19.description: Gentle acoustic folk with layered fingerpicking and light harmonies. The imagery is wide-open spaces — mountains, forests, quiet mornings. A calming, grounding track for nature lovers or winding down.

20.title: Bass Drop Theory
20.description: Aggressive EDM built around a gut-punch bass drop and relentless energy. The track never decelerates — kick after kick at 145 BPM. Built for the most intense moments of a workout or a packed dance floor.
